stories.title: Series Preview — Cubs vs Giants, 3 Games at Wrigley
stories.angle: NEW STORY (Giants series starts today; mandated series-preview slot per is_series_start_today=true)

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Cubs vs. Giants — 3 games at Wrigley starts today. First pitch 1:20 PM CT.

SF went 2-1 vs Milwaukee last week. Cubs (33-30) can't take this series lightly.
Cabrera makes his IL-return start against Robbie Ray.

Wrigley in June. DON'T waste it. 🚩

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Cubs 7, Athletics 6 — Walk-off WIN, PCA Hero and Goat
stories.angle: NEW STORY (June 4 walk-off win — first coverage of this game result)

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
PCA lost the ball in the lights. Then he WON IT at the plate.

Down 6-1 to the A's, the Cubs scored 4 in the 9th.
Crow-Armstrong's bloop single scored Suzuki — Cubs 7, A's 6. Walk-off. 🚩

That's what this Wrigley team does.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Cubs 7, Athletics 6 — Walk-off Comeback
**Status:** NEW STORY (June 4 game result; no coverage yet in pipeline)
**Tier:** 1 (walk-off wins are always Tier 1; "WalkOff" qualifier per playbook)
**Facts:**
- Final score: Cubs 7, Athletics 6 [HIGH — ESPN, ESPN recap, Cubs Insider, ClutchPoints all confirm]
- Cubs trailed 6-1 (at some point entering the late innings — sources say "down 6-1 in the 7th" in one description, another says "down 6-1 entering the 9th") [MEDIUM — slight discrepancy; need to verify exact inning of deficit]
- Cubs scored 4 runs in the 9th inning; had 7 hits in the 9th after only 4 hits in first 8 innings [HIGH — Cubs Insider, ESPN]
- Walk-off: PCA bloop RBI single, scored Seiya Suzuki [HIGH — ClutchPoints, Yahoo Sports confirm specific hit]
- PCA blunder: lost a fly ball in the lights, allowing inside-the-park HR (two runners scored) [HIGH — Yahoo Sports headline "Pete Crow-Armstrong allows inside-the-park homer"; confirmed by multiple sources]
- A's HR-heavy: Shea Langeliers ×2, Todd Soderstrom, Jonah Heim among A's HRs [MEDIUM — AI summary; Langeliers inside-the-park confirmed by sources]
- J.T. Ginn: A's pitcher, 6 IP, 8 K [MEDIUM — search AI summary]
- Cubs record after: 33-30 [HIGH — series-context.json]
- Cubs finished the A's series 2-1 (series win) [HIGH — June 3 was 5-4 loss per story-history; June 4 was 7-6 win]
**CRITICAL FACT TO VERIFY:** Was the deficit "6-1 in the 7th" or "6-1 entering the 9th"? Sources conflict slightly. One says "trailing 6-1 in the 7th" and another says "just four hits in the first eight innings." Using "down 6-1" without specifying the exact inning is safer.
**Freshness:** June 4 game (less than 24 hours old)
**Why it matters:** Walk-offs are peak fan emotion. PCA hero-and-goat narrative is perfect storytelling. Cubs come back from a 5-run deficit on the road... wait, this was at WRIGLEY (home). Yes — Cubs home game. The narrative plays even better.

stories.title: Ben Brown — 2.01 ERA, All-Star Case
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Ben Brown ace narrative; new angle = All-Star case per Sun-Times June 4 article)

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Ben Brown: 2.01 ERA. 47 strikeouts. 44.2 innings. All without any help. 🔥

While Horton had surgery and Boyd was in Iowa rehabbing, Brown was CARRYING this rotation alone.

His All-Star case is already made.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

Cubs sit at 33-30, about 5.5 games behind Milwaukee.
Their June slate has 22 sub-.500 opponents. There's no better stretch to gain ground.

What the Cubs do this month DECIDES the season. ⚾

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Rotation Returns — Cabrera Today, Boyd Final Rehab Saturday
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (rotation health arc; new angle = double-return confirmed: Cabrera starts today, Boyd final Iowa rehab start Saturday then activation)

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
EDWARD CABRERA is starting today for the Cubs — back from a blister IL stint (3-2, 4.00 ERA).

Matthew Boyd makes his final Iowa rehab start Saturday. Activation is coming.

This rotation is no longer on life support. 💪

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Rotation Returns — Cabrera Today, Boyd Saturday
**Status:** FOLLOW UP (rotation health arc; story-history has multiple rotation entries; new angle = confirmed return dates, double-return narrative)
**Tier:** 2
**Facts:**
- Edward Cabrera: 3-2, 4.00 ERA for Cubs in 2026 before IL [HIGH — Bleacher Nation preview, multiple sources]
- Cabrera IL: May 21 (retroactive), blister on right middle finger; 15-day minimum = eligible June 5 [HIGH — multiple reports]
- Cabrera starts Game 1 today [HIGH — multiple preview sources]
- Matthew Boyd: 2025 All-Star, Opening Day starter 2026; meniscus surgery May 2026 [HIGH — Sun-Times]
- Boyd rehab: First start May 31 (4 IP, 2 ER, 63 pitches at Iowa per story-history); second start scheduled Saturday June 7 at Iowa [HIGH — story-history + Bleacher Nation/Yardbarker confirm "two rehab starts before return"]
- Boyd activation: "good to go" after second rehab start per Counsell; activation imminent (likely week of June 8-15) [MEDIUM — Craig Counsell quote from search results]
- Jordan Wicks: optioned back to minors with 15.63 ERA [HIGH — Sun-Times]
- Cole Ferguson: recalled [MEDIUM — Sun-Times]
- Cade Horton: Tommy John surgery, out ~16 months [HIGH — multiple reports]
**Freshness:** Cabrera return information current (June 5 eligible date); Boyd timeline matches story-history entries
**Why it matters:** Rotation depth has been the Cubs' Achilles heel. Double-return of Cabrera + imminent Boyd is the biggest rotation development since Horton went down.

Reports: Cubs have discussed acquiring FREDDY PERALTA from the Mets.

He's a free agent after this season. Counsell managed him in Milwaukee for 5 years.
Even with Cabrera and Boyd returning, this rotation needs another arm.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Cubs-Mets Peralta Trade Buzz
**Status:** NEW STORY (Peralta angle specific to Mets — previous MLB rotation rumors covered without naming Peralta specifically)
**Tier:** 3
**Facts:**
- Reports: Cubs discussed Freddy Peralta with the New York Mets [MEDIUM — Bleacher Nation reports per Bruce Levine; Cubs source denied specific Peralta talks (BN update says "(Or Not?)") — conflict; multiple outlets repeated the report]
- Freddy Peralta: now with the New York Mets (traded from Brewers) [HIGH — multiple sources confirm Mets/Peralta connection]
- Peralta age: 30 (turns 30 in July 2026) [MEDIUM — CBS Sports notes "30 next month" in context of deadline candidates]
- Free agent after 2026 season [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Craig Counsell managed Peralta in Milwaukee, 2019-2023 (overlapped ~5 seasons) [HIGH — confirmed by multiple sources referencing Counsell connection]
- Counsell left Milwaukee after 2023 to join Cubs [HIGH — well-documented]
- Peralta's salary: $15.5M [MEDIUM — Heavy.com headline]
- Mets situation: bad record (entering play at 14-23 at one point); potential sellers at deadline [MEDIUM — CBS Sports, SI]
- Robbie Ray (Giants starter today) also mentioned as Cubs trade target [MEDIUM — search results; noted as irony angle but too speculative for today's tweet]
**Freshness:** Trade reports from May 8 (Bleacher Nation) plus multiple subsequent reports. Not 24-hour-fresh strictly, but: (a) Tier 3 story; (b) ongoing rumor arc; (c) Cubs still 33-30 and needing rotation help with active context
**Note:** Flag as reports/rumors only. Do not state as fact that talks occurred definitively. Use hedge language.
**Why it matters:** Cubs fans tracking deadline moves; Counsell connection makes this compelling even as a rumor; Peralta is a high-upside rental who could push Cubs into October.
